app.name: Prompt-Driven Development (PDD)
app.one_liner: An AI-powered toolkit for automated code generation, testing, and maintenance driven by GitHub issues and prompt-based workflows.

screens.name: PDD Web Interface
screens.purpose: Visual management of PDD projects, including issue implementation, prompt execution, and project state monitoring.
primary_actions: Generate and test code from prompts

rust_commands.name: pdd_bug
rust_commands.purpose: Create failing tests for bugs reported in GitHub issues.

rust_commands.name: pdd_change
rust_commands.purpose: Implement feature requests from GitHub issues using a 12-step agentic workflow.

rust_commands.name: pdd_fix
rust_commands.purpose: Fix failing tests generated by `pdd bug` with traceable validation against user symptoms and cleanup steps.

rust_commands.name: pdd_generate
rust_commands.purpose: Generate architecture.json from a PRD-style GitHub issue using an 11-step workflow.

rust_commands.name: pdd_sync
rust_commands.purpose: Automate the complete development cycle with intelligent decision-making, real-time visual feedback, and sophisticated state management based on prompts.

rust_commands.name: pdd_test
rust_commands.purpose: Generate comprehensive UI tests from issue descriptions using an 18-step workflow including exploratory testing, contract validation, and accessibility audits.

mvp_plan: week 1: Establish prompt-to-code sync mechanism with commit validation
week 1: Implement core CLI commands (`pdd change`, `pdd bug`, `pdd fix`) with basic GitHub issue integration
week 1: Set up local web interface at localhost:9876 for project visualization
acceptance_tests: Step 6 FIX_LOCATIONS in the orchestrator are verified deterministically via grep to ensure precise AI-applied changes.
The `pdd sync` command maintains bidirectional consistency between architecture.json and code includes, detecting and preventing silent sync-order divergence.
When a user runs `pdd bug <issue-url>`, the system creates a failing test using the real artifact from the bug report, not a synthetic stand-in.
When a user runs `pdd change <issue-url>`, the system implements the feature via a 12-step workflow and commits all referenced prompt files.
When a user runs `pdd fix <issue-url>`, the system traces the proposed fix forward to the original symptom and includes a cleanup step.
